title: Diagnose a persistent cache miss
description: A procedure to find why an action never hits the cache by isolating the input that changes between builds.
body: Use when remote or local cache hit rates are unexpectedly low. Do not accept slow rebuilds without finding the cause. Steps: enable execution-log or --explain to see why an action re-ran; compare the action's inputs and command line across two builds; look for absolute paths, timestamps, environment variables, or undeclared inputs that differ. Validation signals include identifying the volatile input and a stable action key after the fix. Risk boundary: a non-deterministic action invalidates the cache for everyone downstream, so fix the source of variance rather than disabling caching.
